reviews.review_text: Highly recommend staying here, we were in the unpowered section, view of the spectacular ranges, friendly staff, great amenities. walking tracks, mountain biking, and close enough to explore Wilpena Pound. Rawnsley Station was a highlight of the Flinders Ranges for us.

My wife and I have just finished a four day stay at Rawnsley Park Station in our caravan. We had an awesome time from start to finish. On our second night we walked to the Woolshed Restaurant for Happy Hour and ended up staying for dinner as there had been a cancellation, the service was excellent the food amazing and to finish the evening off the very kind manager Kate arranged a lift for us back to the caravan park, thankyou Kate. On our last day we did the all day Flinders Rangers all day tour with Craig, what an amazing day words just dont do the views justice. Thankyou for a awesome day Craig. The facilities at Rawnsley Park Station are very good and maintained well. If you are planning on coming to the Flinders Rangers, I would highly recommend you stay at Rawnsley Park Station you won't be sorry. Thankyou to all the staff at Rawnsley, my wife and I had an awesome stay and we will be back one day. Cheers Peter

Rawsley Park Sheep Station was so amazing.  We did bush camping in caravan down by the dam which overlooked Rawsley Bluff.  Was amazing spot we had for 6 nights.  We had the Lamb Platter for 2 first night at there Woolshed Restaurant.  Best lamb ever had so much flavour & tender as can be.  We need up buying some of the reception areas frozen lamb packs .  The Saltbush Lamb Sausages are a treat too.  Saw some wildlife & a few of their sheep.  Did some drives & walks.  Highly recommend Pigist Lookout drive & Moralana Scenic Drive too .  Spectacular scenery & lookout location.  We did Wilpena Pound Campground too just  down the road but Rawsley Park Station 30 KMs away is a MUST GO TO EXPERIENCE also.
At the foot of fantastic flinders ranges and AWD access to gorges. Helpful managers. Gold coin donation for gerni to wash off locusts
Love this place. It has everything on the Station and everything else not far away.

Incredible caravan park! We stayed in the unpowered site and drove around 10-15 minutes into the bush camp area and the views were incredible. Powered sites were crowded and seemed close together from what I could see. Staff were friendly. We only used the toilet and showers one time and they were clean. Laundry was good. We went to the restaurant once, staff were friendly and food was good for the takeaway menu. Stayed twice, 1 night Dec 2025 and 4 nights April 2026.
Great scenery greener than normal due to large rainfal lately

Camped with the fam for two nights at an unpowered site. Plenty of room and the views are spectacular. The facilities are super clean and maintained thoroughly! Great spot to camp and have a camp fire, highly recommended!
Good site to stay for Flinders excursions. Unfortunately wifi not working but no big deal...good amenities, often cleaned but some people are just grubs.
Toilet blocks and camp kitchen clean and well maintained
Stunning spot for camping.  Beautiful and peaceful with clean amenities
Very nice place to spend the night, clean and well kept kitchen and bathroom. Had a letter waiting for me so made it easy for me to arrive after hours

Great campground to stay in the Flinders! Pool was always clean, amenities were always clean and customer service was second to none. Informative and always greeted with a smile. Will be back in the future.
Walks were beautiful, 4wd tracks were great
Tolle Blick auf die Wilpena Pounds. Viele Wanderungen von Rawnsley aus möglich. Einkaufsmöglichkeit und Restaurant vorhanden.
Great eco villas. Great scenery
Great park in the Flinders, no real feel of a working station.

One of the memories I'll take away is sitting on the porch watching the sunrise and sunset turn the whole landscape different colours. Loved the location and the facilities were excellent. I thought it was pretty good value too.
Oasis in the Flinders Ranges Clean, nice staff, provide scenic flight

We did two tours at Rawnsley Park Station — Sunset on the Chase and Bunyeroo & Brachina Gorges — and both were excellent.

We saw a huge amount of wildlife, including kangaroos, wallabies, and wallaroos, along with a family of emus and several wedge-tailed eagles. Being able to see so much wildlife in its natural setting was a real highlight.

Our guide, Georgia, was great. She was very knowledgeable about the area and shared a lot of context about the landscape, geology, and animals, while keeping the tours relaxed and enjoyable.

The Sunset on the Chase tour was a nice way to end the day. The drinks and canapés at sunset were well done and added a relaxed finish after a full day of exploring.

Overall, both tours were well run and worth doing if you’re visiting the Flinders Ranges

Awesome outback units! Very helpful and friendly staff. No reason not to give them 5 stars. Above all, great location. The hiking tracks were easily outlined and they both had a mix of easy to difficult tracks.
Great place to visit,rooms great,food was great,staff were great

A stunning cosy apartment with the most spectacular outback view that you can get at Flinders Ranges. All of the mountains encompass you and the sunsets and sunrises are mystical. They also give you a lot of complimentary foods like lolly and even their own coffee roast. Would definitely recommend if you happen to come to Flinders Ranges.
Sehr schöner Ort mit sehr gut ausgestatteten kleinen Ferienhäusern. Gut ausgestattete Küche  so dass man selber kochen kann. Wir haben auch den Pool genutzt. Es war wirklich sehr schön.

Fantastic stay! The excursions are good and our guide, Rachel (Rach) and Damon were very informative and excellent drivers. Made a good cup of tea as well 😉
Stunning views 🤩
Motel mit Westernromantik, leider war das Wetter sehr durchwachsen.

Always love coming up to this place.
Home is where the heart is.
10/10
Great place to stay.
Views to download for. If you climb the mountain, you may just do that 😂. Wilderness camping or full service. Must try the restaurant too. Emu's and roos Galore.

We have just returned from a 5 night stay at the Rawnsley Park Station Campground. Found the facilities of top quality. With a small shop, clean and tidy ablution blocks to top this off there is a enclosed camp kitchen with 2 barbecues table and chairs, microwave, lounge chairs and air-conditioning. Which was great as we struck a few hot days. The sites are a bit up and down which is expected. If you have mobility problems it may pay to ask for a site on the flatter areas.
This is a good spot to set up and discover the other areas in the Flinders Ranges.

Whilst we liked this camping spot, the design is a little flawed. Everyone is forced to drive past the powered sites when they arrive or return so there is little peace to be had, especially in the afternoon. Amenities are good and well maintained. There are numerous excellent walks and drives to do nearby. Its a great location, and convenient as a base whilst exploring the Flinders, just factor in the dust and noise from the cars driving past.

Great unpowered camping sites.. Loads of space and feels like free camping but costs haha!
Many spots with great views of the range. Heaps of emus, a bunch of sheep and a few kangaroos to keep you company.

Amazing place to camp. So much land, even when it's busy you can find a quiet spot. The views are spectacular! Make your own fire pit.  Lots of great hikes from the station. Heaps of Emus and sheep. Lots of bird life. We really enjoyed our stay.

Rawnsley Park Station is one of the most beautiful places in Australia. The views are absolutely stunning and the whole area just takes you to your happy place the moment you arrive. We love it here — peaceful, relaxing and simply unforgettable.

This place boasts everything you could ask for to enjoy your stay whilst using as a base camp for exploring. Bonus.. has it's own exclusive trails, check out Rawnsley bluff is a must !!
Super friendly staff...fuel, car wash bay and supplies with ample stock to purchase. Wood can be purchased as well.
Pool and large undercover area as well as large community kitchen. Washrooms coin operated. 2 amenity blocks.
Definitely worth staying at.
Absolutely loved it!!
Well set out, clean, good services

A real surprise. 1st thing, it is quite large but well catered for. Spotless amenities blocks, large laundry facility and lots of drying area. Did I say spotless? Amenities block cleaned daily. Rubbish bins emptied daily. Great site store with essential goods plus fuel is available and many powered sites. As far as I could tell all powered sites were nearly level and big enough to take a large van and monster truck. Lots of shade and the area is stunning. There are fire pits dotted around for use out of fire ban season, byo or purchase firewood at the store.Lots of walks, trails and wildlife to see. A large communal kitchen with slow combustion heater for the winter is also there making this a great site for winter travellers.

This is in stunning country, a stay at Rawnsley Park Station Campground is a great way to see it.  This visit we are in a campground cabin, as we are staying for more than a week and need the flexibility to get around the area.  It's terrific.

It is always an outstanding experience staying at Rawnsley Park Station in the magnificent Flinders Rangers, and this day was no less delightful. ￼ Dinner and the on-site restaurant is always a really pleasurable experience, though the wine list could do with a little help.
A Great Less Crowded place than Wilpena Pound to stay.

Overall, it was a very nice stay. The room was clean and perfect for anyone looking to relax. Unfortunately, the weather wasn’t good during our visit, so we couldn’t drive on the 4WD track we were really looking forward to exploring.

Clean, comfortable (great beds) and spacious units situated in a beautiful landscape. Personable and helpful staff, good walking trails and well-located for exploring the southern and central Flinders Ranges. All-in-all we would say Rawnsley Park Station exceeded our expectations.
Amazing view from the cottages nestled in the Australian outback bush setting! They did a good job preserving the nature yet creating an enjoyable experience! Highly recommended!
Wow

Wonderfull stay right at the foot of the ranges. What more do you need?
Stayed a couple of night in the single room accommodation. Perfect for non campers if you want to hike the Flinders. Comfy bed, hot shower, kitchen, BBQ, fire pits.

Would recommend as a base to visit  the Flinders Ranges,  helpful staff, even the maintenance  man who assisted us to change our tyre. Did a 4 WD experience , our driver Shane was awesome, his knowledge of wildlife and plants exceptional he even served us drinks and a platter watching a beautiful sunset. DINNER AT THE Woolshed restaurant was next level would thoroughly recommend  this place

Amazing...!!! Beautiful place, nice accommodations..We stayed in a 2 bedroom cottage.
Clean, fully equipped kitchen. Nice bedrooms with comfortable beds. Nice comforters and sheets. Each room had a split A/C system. Lots of room for luggage.
Nice T.V., Satellite, plenty of channels. Wifi was decent.
Small deck in the front. Great for watching a sunrise or a wine/ beer in the afternoon.
Nice pool area. Kangaroos and Emus out your front door.
We did take a 4.5 hr morning tour with Sean...OMG...Sean...glad it was only 1/2 day because my head was about to explode with all the information this guy provided..BOOM..!!! I mean like 2 semesters of college in 1/2 day. He was great...heck he had us listening to a tree ..!! Who does that..?
Highly recommend doing a tour. And if you get Sean as a guide...strap yourself in..!!
Great place, highly recommend...

Hatten zuerst 1 Nacht ein Cabin mit 2 Schlafräumen. Gebaut 2018. Sehr gut für 3 Sterne. Alles was man braucht. Haben dann gegen Aufpreis noch 2 Nächte eine Eco Villa gehabt. Die meisten sind aus 2006. Die fast 20 Jahre sind schon zu merken. Allerdings ist es sehr komfortabel und ruhig etwas auf dem Berg gelegen. Optimal für mehr Privatsphäre als in den Cabins. Es gab dann auch Brot, Eier und Saft etc. für 2x Frühstück. Ich finde, der Aufpreis hat sich gelohnt.
Während Sonnenaufgang und Sonnenuntergang kann man viele Kängurus beobachten.

Large station with large camping area
Dog friendly
Dumppoint, water, power, laundry
Bore water
Lots of Unpowered sites
Shop in reception area, basic supplies
Car wash
Can do self- drives on station, however payable
Great walking tracks around the station
Great base to visit the Flinders

This place is not well run sites and roads are not maintained.
Staff are rude and all the owners are interested in is getting money out of you.

We stayed in the unpowered sites. It was a little chaotic at check in as someone was learning but the unpowered sites section was lovely -so much nicer than I was expecting. And so pleasantly surprised to have the toilets in the unpowered sites section which were well maintained. We ate at the restaurant with a party of 4 and that was a lovely experience. My meal was pasta and it was delicious. The lamb shank was just ok and the sirloin was gristly. The atmosphere and service was excellent. I was very impressed with the signage for the walk as we went up to the Rawnsley Bluff. Absolutely essential that the signposting was clear (you dont want people going over the edge or getting lost) - and it was exactly that - with painted yellow and white stripes. So thank you for keeping on top of those hike markers. Will be back!
Amazing drive in camp ground and facilities were very good
Went for the station outback experience in the amazing Flinders Ranges  and was not disappointed.
Great park, good warm amenities and the Woolshed Restaurant was great.  It is 20klm from the Wilpena Pound info centre.
We loved our 3 day stay at Rawnsley Station. We had an amazing unpowered site further in the park all by ourselves. It felt like an American movie set. Absolutely stunning.

A great camp ground,  great facilities, and central for easy access into National Park.  Alot more space than Wilpena Pound (which we found very busy and crowded), and all sites have fire pit.  General store has the basics, diesel available too.  Highly recommend the restaurant but you'll need to book in to get a table.
Plenty of things to explore with walks, bike tracks and 4wd all within Rawnsley.  The airfield is here too for easy access for scenic flights.  It's also dog friendly, and you can arrange dog sitting if you need to leave your fur friend for the day.

Very disappointing
We had been looking forward to this for months
We booked a site to suit a large car and van and even called back to confirm we could fit the car beside the van with the awning out.
“Yes our sites are wide so you can drive through pull the awning out and have room to park the car beside it”
We rock up and are allocated a site that we can barely get the awning out let alone park the car beside it.
We went back to reception to see if we could be reallocated and the only substitute offered was not suitable.
It is such a shame to have looked forward to the flinders for months and called back multiple times to confirm that we would fit properly only to not fit.
We drove past free camps on the way that would have been better than what we have paid for.

We have a 21' van and large vehicle and were given a smaller powered site across from the rubbish bins giving us little room to manoeuvre. Stayed 3 nights and during that time we saw many larger sites empty the entire time. There were rules in the powered site area about setting up your own camp fires as there were many throughout the park already in place, which we were happy with. Our adjacent site set up their own fire pit within a metre of our car. I did speak to the office staff about this and nothing was done. This put a dampener on an overall good trip.
We only drove to check out the facilities - the kitchen is spacious, the toilet is clean as if newly renovated. we did not stay as we felt it was crowded and was tol dry for me (barren somewhat).
Great place to base ourselves to see the Flinders Ranges. Close to Wilpena Pound and an easy drive to other features. Plenty of walks and drives for both on and off-road vehicles.

Beautiful setting, level sites for caravans. Clean amenities block. Diesel fuel available at a realistic price. An enjoyable stay, and a 20 km drive to Wilpena for many trailheads was not an issue.
Drinking water at Rawnsley Camoground only available from the rain tanks, and to fill a 500ml bottle takes 5 minutes. Patience required.
Very good caravan park for bush camping.
Great location to explore Flinders Ranges from. Well laid out with excellent amenities.

Always a great place to stay and now seems to have decent mobile phone service and improved WiFi. Office has recently been redone after about 30 years and you can still buy basic supplies, beer, wine and local Gin there.
A great place to stay while investing the area around Wilpena Pound. Definitely try the restaurant and also consider a flight while you are there.
Amazing views and very good facilities. Overall very nice stay.

Loved the location and the concept. Execution lacking.  Eco-Villas cheaply furnished and ours was unclean (dirty crockery and pans, dirty inside drawers, dirty bins…I could go on). Reception area shabby (eg plant pots with their sales labels still on). So many wonderful opportunities overlooked. This place could be stunning with the right care and thought put into it.Overall, for us very disappointing.

Specracular views.  Abundant wildlife. Comfortable cabin, although all of the drinking glasses were dirty with orange pulp. Guest wifi was a bit patchy. Many of the nearby walking trails are 'adequately' marked.

Wow, what a fantastic stay we had. A spacious, comfortable and neat accommodation. Including a small swimming pool, WiFi, satellite tv, and laundry facilities. Well made with all supplies available. Restaurant with good food on side. Walking distance trails and lookout points, would stay here again. Would visit again

Nice spot for family camping mainly. So handy to have this convenience store inside the camp where you find those needed stuff that you could have forgotten. You can see animals in their environment which is lovely. Good toilets and amenities and a it is a mix between caravan and bush camping. Also near to parks to walk and see amazing sunsets/sunrise. Really loved this spot.

Best value, location is has everything you need at the caravan park.  Clean toilets and showers, fuel, convenient.  One of the best caravan parks I have been too, plus the view is spectacular from the campsite.

Great location with a range of different accomodation options. Our 1 bed cabin was comfortable and access to the swimming pool was a huge plus given the temperature. Plus you get Emus visiting you at breakfast.

5 nights in the Tourist Park , Powered site which was level and well drained, If you are looking for a quiet relaxing outback caravan park with a short drive to amazing walks and scenery this is it , Managers Nathan and Katie will go out of their way to help with walks and drives , Fuel is well priced and the Office has some basic food and drink supplies ,
This place is awesome, the location scenery and people
Super schön dort. Der Bungalow war einfach, aber mit allem ausgestattet. Bisschen in die Jahre gekommen, aber alles in allem 👍
We stayed in the unpowered area of the caravan park. And it was great 😃 the managers and staff were helpful when required the location perfect
Took a flight from the airfield at the sheep station didn’t really see the venue
A working sheep station with a great restaurant and close to walking treks and 4wd track

Just WOW!!! We stayed here mid August 24 and had 4 nights in a cabin. They're clean, well kept and everything you need for a comfortable stay. The property itself has a lot to see and do, waking trails, 4WD tours, helicopter and plane rides, swimming pool, caravan park with shop, fuel, alcohol. The Woolshed restaurant is superb 👌🏻 We did a sunset tour with Sean as our guide. He was clearly experienced and knew the area well. He was genuine and friendly and made our night equally memorable as the views. I felt safe with him behind the wheel. Highly recommend doing at least one of the tours Rawnsley offers, it was the highlight of our trip to the Flinders. We certainly will be back.
Peaceful and beautiful in the bush camping section. Heaps of sights with spectacular views of Rawnsley Bluff. Great place to base yourself when visiting the southern part of the Flinders Ranges.

Amazing place to stay. The staff were really friendly and helpful. Great facilities,  modern and clean. The powered caravan sites  were a generous size and very level.
We enjoyed the nearby walking trails,  mountain bike track and had fun on the 4WD track. We will definitely go back again.
Great campgrounds powered and huge unpowered area. Great amenities are a short drive. 🔥allowed. Fuel,firewood available. Awesome views.

入住一個自帶廚房的小屋 兩人一晚$220
床非常舒服 浴室有足夠大的空間 有一房一個車位有屋簷遮擋
器具齊全的廚房可以自己準備食物（記得先買好食材）
最棒的是夕陽的山景 還可以看到月出
有很多羊在附近吃草
Absolutely loved our stay in one of the superior cabins. They have perfect views of Rawnsley bluff and The chase. Sunsets and dawn were amazing and there is no shortage of kangaroos and emus around.
Five night stay with family was very enjoyable. The park walks are great and the food at the Woodshed restaurant was excellent. The children were sad when our stay came to an end.
Very relaxing stay, great meal at the restaurant and multiple rounds of delicious desserts!!
Attentive, clean, spacious and comfortable
Beautiful scapes. Comfy room fully equipped.
WOW, lovely food, great atmosphere, wonderful staff. A fantastic experience 😁

We stayed in the caravan park and would thoroughly recommend Rawnsley Park Station as a base for your exploration of the Flindets Ranges. Fantastic new amenities complement level sites, camp kitchen and BBQ area. Staff are super friendly and helpful.

We had a very nice stay.
The reception was very friendly and helpful.
The apartment had a lot of space and everyone had their own parking space right next to it. Very well equipped kitchen and spacious bathroom.
As a small highlight, there was a swimming pool on site.
There were many activities on offer and we had a really nice and entertaining informative tour in a 4WD off-road vehicle.
Had an amazing experience here. Rooms, service, restaurant, pool and location were all incredible. No complaints whatsoever!
Its a gem
Wonderful spot
Good room, comfy bed, big shower, adequate kitchen facilities. Decent pool and restaurant on site.

Great place, did I say GREAT PLACE.
You are with nature, kangaroos, emus, birds. Can't fault the restaurant here. Can't fault anything.

Fabulous location. Sunrise and sunset on the Bluff are an event in their own right. Room was comfortable, pool was great. Very well marked trails around the station with magnificent views. Suiting different levels of hiking ability. Good restaurant on the station also.

Endroit vraiment superbe.
Le bungalow pourrait-être rendu un peu plus charmant, en particulier en améliorant l'éclairage (vraiment moche).
Le lit était vraiment excellent.

Assez déçus en revanche par le restaurant (voir critique séparée)
Had great two nights stay during the long weekend at their powered campsite.
Very comfortable, clean rooms. The staff are very friendly and helpful. Great location to base yourself to explore the Flinders Ranges.

I was staying in an unpowered site in beautiful Rawnsley Station caravan park with mountain backdrop, surrounded by wildlife (kangaroos, wallabies, emus, birds). A stunning view to wake up to! The staff were extremely friendly, helpful and accomodating, with lots of great advice on the best ways to explore the area. DOG FRIENDLY!! My Kelpie stayed in the kennel for a day so I could explore the National park, and was very happy. Staff were very helpful even when I arrived back much later than anticipated. Very clean and easily ammenities, including showers, communal kitchen (with BBQ, stoves, microwave, kettle etc) laundry, water available, a small grocer with lamb available from the Station, and a petrol bowser to fill up big days of driving.
Great experience for a first-time visit. Will be back.  Stayed in Eco Villas and were very impressed.  All tours were well worth it and a must do. The only disappointment was the woodshed restaurant.

Totally different pictures of rooms and the view differs from room to room. The rooms seems bigger and updated in photos. Clean rooms but very dated and they are Cabins not apartments.Disappointed for the money’s worth.
Great way to escape for a while from everyday living.
I traveled down from QLD and visited Rawnsley Park Station. I stayed in an eco villa, which has a kitchen so we could self-cater. It was a very nice place to stay, and had fantastic views.
Brilliant place to base yourself with in the Flinders Ranges.  The woodshed restaurant has a superb  menu, a lovely bar, and fantastic staff who are always happy to help.
Very clean, comfortable cabins in a very clean site.  Staff were wonderful.  The Woolshed restaurant food and service was amazing.  All set in a stunning location.

We loved our stay on a powered caravan site at Rawnsley Park Station. Great welcome on arrival. Good amenities and can purchase firewood. Powered sites have either individual or shared campfire areas. Fuel on site. Great views to Rawnsley Bluff. Hikes and bike rides on Station.  Unpowered sites available close to amenities or pick a spot over a huge area if you want a private bush camp experience.

We stayed in a 1-bedroom cabin offering comfortable, no-frills accommodation ideally suited for outdoor enthusiasts. The cabin is clean and well-maintained, with effective air conditioning and a quiet setting conducive to rest after active days.

The kitchenette is functional and adequately equipped for self-catering, though cookware shows signs of wear. The cabin's positioning provides direct access to premium hiking trails, with Rawnsley Bluff offering exceptional panoramic views of the ranges and is particularly striking at dawn and dusk.

Staff demonstrated professionalism and attentiveness throughout our stay. The on-site Woolshed Restaurant provides quality dining options for guests preferring not to self-cater.

Rawnsley Park Station functions effectively as a strategic base for exploring the Flinders Ranges wilderness. Recommended for those prioritising location and outdoor access over resort-style amenities, altough they do have eco-cabin options.

Powered Site / Caravan Park
Well maintained & organised sites
Caravan Park ammenities are adequate / clean & tidy / well stocked little store / fuel prices are decent.

Highlight: SUNSET on CHACE tour
Absolutely phenomenal experience
Spectacular 360• scenery as you take in the sunset atop one of the peaks of the Arkapena track. The 4WD trek to get there was memorable, taking in the pastoral lands and rugged terrain. Craig our tour guide was fantastic, providing relevant commentary & conversation and well organised hosting the sunset drinks & nibbles.

Rawnsley Park Station has once again exceeded our expectations !!!
Great spot good facilities
Scouts honour this place is amazing xxx Just do it.

Rawnsley Park Caravan Park and Cabins staff are 5 star. The cabin was clean and even if a bit dated, everything worked and was comfortable. Shared facilities are good and clean. The restaurant's staff attitude was matter of fact, no warmth or care, hence 4 stars. Food was too expensive for the quality.

Beautiful place situated on the south face of Wilpena Pound, you can hike/run up to the top of Rawnsley Bluff in the dark to get a gorgeous sunrise…

Staff are really friendly, helpful and knowledgeable, and their 4WD track is a great half day adventure.
Staying in  bush camp out back

We stayed 7 nights on a powered van site at RPS. It was fantastic! We loved everything about it. The location was ideal for 7 days of sightseeing in the stunning Fliners Ranges. The Woolshed restaurant was great. Our site was private enough, yet conveniently located for all services including the pool. Highly recommended. We will return.

This place is amazing, the scenery, the serenity, the service, the accommodation, the food, the friendly staff the whole experience was something everyone should experience.
I can't recommend this place highly enough.

Camped at Rawnsley Park. Great views, very friendly, helpful staff. The facilities from kitchen to bathroom/showers & laundry were very clean & well looked after. Definitely be happy to stay again. The restaurant is very nice, great food & reasonably priced.

We had a fantastic time at Rawnsley Park Station.  We stayed in the bush camp section and accessed a great spot with amazing views.  The facilities are close by and very well maintained.  We used the Station as a base to explore the Wilpena area including the Parachilna and Brachina gorges.  It's close to the road so no airing down required for your van.  There is water accessible on the way in, whilst they say this is bore water and slightly saline I couldn't taste the difference and would happily use this water in my van.  We filled our tanks before we arrive from Hawker.  The Woolshed restuarant on site provides a limited takeaway menu and the Vader pizza with a serve of wedges was a great night off from cooking.  This was one of our top two station stays whilst in the Flinders Ranges and we'll be back to see the region during the wildflower season some time soon I'm sure!  Jarret and Lyn - Gecko Adventures Australia.
Rawnsley Park Station is a very special place with great facilities; beautiful walking trails and friendly staff.

We booked 3 nights at Rawnsley Park station campground over Easter. It was the worst camping experience in our 3 week trip around South Australia. There were hundreds of campers. Some parties were forced to camp a couple of metres from the the 4WD tracks with 4WD traffic roaring past their ears constantly. There was little shade and no facilities offered like picnic tables around the campground.  There were a total of only 7 shower cubicles for women which resulted in terribly long queues and extremely dirty floors. There was only one camp kitchen with one fridge, one sink and two stove tops to service the hundreds of people. People were getting in each other's way and feeling stressed while preparing their meals. We checked out a day early, happy to forefeit a day's camping fees to find a more serene campground.
Great sites with beautiful views. Clean amenities. Staff were very helpful in choosing site. Lots of interesting places to visit for a day. Hikes/walks close by

A pretty setting, with some colourful birds. The room was clean and the bed comfortable. Furnishings a bit dated and layout didn't really work. The only place to watch the TV was from the bed. The sofa was too small to be comfortable and facing the sink.
WiFi was good. Staff polite and someone came to help with the TV and aircon, neither of which were obvious to use.

Great location to all things|Campground good though some sites a little close together. Great area for self contained camping|Great having dog kennels to enable trips into NP|Facilities good|Woolshed restaurant was great. Fabulous meal plus enjoyed happy hour

Had marvellous stay here.Staff are friendly & very helpful.Park itself is very scenic lot's of walks to do.Lovely Woolshed Restaurant.|Views of Flinders Ranges,variety of Accommodation.Caravan Park is great.Good as a base to explore.
Stayed in caravan site. Toilet block and camp kitchen clean and well kept

I recently stayed at Rawnsley Park Station and had a fantastic time. The location is stunning, the accommodation was comfortable, and the whole experience was incredibly relaxing. I’d definitely recommend it to anyone visiting the area.
Loved it. Really calm and relaxing couple of days. Definitely reccomend the woolshed for dinner.
Great scenery, very good facilities. Lots of space. Clean. Most campers are quiet and considerate, with the odd exception. Bush camping is good value.

The weather was looking inclement so we decided to spend the extra $$$ on the Eco Villa. So glad we did.. Our first. Night in the Superior Villa was fine but the Eco Villa was amazing. ||Private, quiet, amazing views (when the clouds lifted), great shower and bath and so comfortable.

Rawnsley Park Station (RPS) has numerous overnight stay options from bush camping to eco-lodge/cabins.||The station is expansive! Amenities in the campground are modern, clean and tidy.||RPS office also serves as a small shop/kiosk. Fuel is available.||RPS have several hikes on the station, some that include views of Wilpena Pound. RPS is located approximately 20 minutes drive south of Wilpena Pound hikes - within Ikara-Flinders Ranges National Park. Nominal NP fees for vehicles and hikers apply.||During wet weather (which we encountered in March 2026) there is a floodwater to navigate to enter the RPS, located on a gravel road with several livestock grates.||There is a restaurant on-site that we would also recommend ‘The Woolshed’; bookings need to be made in advance. The customer service was great!||Scenic flights are available.||One improvement I would recommend to RPS is to staff The Woolshed for a period of time in the morning so that visitors to the station can purchase a takeaway coffee. The closest espresso coffee is 75kms north in Blinman at The Miners Crib Cafe (or 35kms south in Hawker).
Great experience with spectacular scenery and beautiful walks

Loved our stay in one of the Eco villas. Everything you needed was there, including a full fridge to make your own breakfast. Loved the skylight in the bedroom so you could see the stars at night.  We did 2 tours, sunset on the Chase and a tour of Flinders Rangers, with Georgie, her knowledge and enthusiasm was wonderful.  Did the short flight which gave us another aspect and view of the rangers.  |Everything was perfect, staff friendly and made our stay there feel special.
Great camping spot. Quiet and relaxing Staff are exceptional from counter to the outside people. Amenities over the top.
We stayed at RPS for four nights. |The place is beautiful. Remote and a definite sense of being in the outback.|Excellent service and food. |Would definitely return and highly recommend.

Unfortunately due to the complete closure of the Flinders Ranges NP, multiple road closures and flood affected roads we had to make the decision to cancel our planned trip to Rawnsley Park Station.  Our planned day trip booked had been amended due to NP closure for pest eradication however the floods caused the excursion to be completely cancelled.  Rawnsley Park still expected us to make the journey across flood impacted roads in a hire car and then to stay two days with no access to the NP .  They indicated that perhaps some roads would be open.  They kept our $200 deposit.  This is a very inflexible approach considering the impacts of flooding.  If we do eventually make it to Flinders Ranges we will stay elsewhere.
Vi var på 3 4WD ture med Georgia. Det var en meget stor oplevelse. Georgias viden og humør er helt i top. Vi kan kun anbefale disse ture og hende som Guide. Wauuuugh
Peaceful place and a lot of activities

We stayed in our van. One of the amenities block wasn't working. Was very dusty. There were some shade trees which was good.

Had dinner at the restaurant. Was delicious food but small portions.

Would stay somewhere else when we return to Flinders Ranges

Excellent camp ground nestled amongst natural vegetation with the best views overlooking Flinders Rangers. We did two tours and the staff were amazing and were really flexible and great with my son. Georgia our tour guide was outstanding. New titled bathrooms were a nice touch too.

My family and I just stayed here for the fourth time in two years and absolutely loved our stay as always.

Bee at reception was lovely and welcoming and Kate, the General Manager, seemed to be everywhere at the same time to ensure guests were always attended to.

We stayed in one of the Superior Holiday Cabins: spotless, very nicely decorated and with a full kitchen. The views from here are nothing short of amazing. We enjoyed the BBQ area and the pool.

Information on exploring the area is very comprehensive and enabled us to do exciting hikes and beautiful scenic drives.

Thank you to all who made our stay so special, we'll be back for a fifth time and many times after!
This is a really fantastic place service is very good wonderful staff

My partner and I planned a trip to flinders coming from central Victoria not knowing anything about the area, we booked the bush camp unpowered site and once we arrived we were greeted by the friendly staff who gave us a map which was very easy to follow, they also provided a pamphlet with places to go, tracks to drive and the surrounding towns with pet friendly options. To say that we are absolutely amazed with this place would be a huge understatement.
If you are considering coming to flinders ranges this is the best place 100%
We got takeaway from the Woolshed Restaurant and the food was unbelievable.
We would absolutely recommend this and will be coming back.
Ps. If you are going unpowered go out to the bush camping as it is worth it.

Hi Readers, Spectacular is the first word I uttered when I went to our unpowered site. This station ( facility ) is spread in thousands of acres facing the Flinder ranges. While coming from the Hawker, take the route of Flinder ranges way to enter this property, neatly 25 min of drive from Hawker town centre. Remember, the road of flinders Ranges is the most happening road like lookouts, short or long hiking walk, scenic drives so worth staying here. This property is with powered , unpowered, cabins, bush camping, private accomodations with friendly staff. You will not be disappointed whether you choose bush camping because the alloyed bush camping area with flush toilet facility too; I must say you would definately love unpowered or bush camping area ( $34) in the hilly and rolling mountains of Rawnsley Park station. This station or facility is with communal shower, flush toilets including fuel, general food store ( onsite the office) , car wash, air compressor, dedicated bin arrangements everywhere, swimming pool, drinking water, breakfast -lunch- dinner facility on demand with charges, short and long walking / hiking trails which is within the Rawnsley. Every soot We stayed for 2 nights overlooking view of Flinder ranges, under open starry night sky. A lot of things to do nearby and around the Flinder ranges. You will not be disappointed. If you are short of grocery ( fresh fruit or veg)  then you can buy from Wilpena station which is also another park station for travellers. Obviously, grocery prices is higher. We visited the Flinder ranges during summer Break but ideal season is April to September. Hope this information helpful; Thanks.
Fantastic location and service, if you want to camp away from the crowds and like hikes this is the place for you

Intended to stay 2 days and extended to 4.  Very friendly staff, clean and functional amenities.  Great location looking at the south east end of the range.  Great area to explore.  I have met 5 couples travelling through and we have had a few drinks and plenty of laughs.  Can't speak mote highly of the place. P.S. Fuel at the staiton is cheaper than Hawker.  Bonus.
all servce
Bit overpriced but nice rooms and location.
Mit mit an da gewesen, eialig schön gelegen, viele Fliegen ( Kopfnetz unbedingt vorher kaufen!!). Alles sauber, ab 22:00 absolut ruhig, am Wochenende aber voll, dann besser vorbuc hen!!

We bush camped here for 3 days while we explored the area. Rawnsley Park Station was a fabulous location. The views and campsites were spectacular. We have 2 dogs travelling with us and were able to  pay for them to be in the kennels while we went to the national park locations.  The price for an unpowered site for our  caravan was cheaper than some others we have stayed at with 100% more! Would highly recommend when visiting the Flinders Ranges.

I was torn between staying at Rawnsley Park Station and Wilpena Pound Resort Caravan Park, and chose Rawnsley based on the Google reviews. However, after our stay, I honestly wish we’d gone with Wilpena instead.

The customer service was minimal and unwelcoming, which was really disappointing. The camping spots and park directions weren’t clear, and the facilities were not well maintained. The bathroom sinks were smelly and clearly hadn’t been cleaned properly, with stagnant water in the overflow. The showers and toilets also needed more regular cleaning and attention.

The camp kitchen was another letdown, there were no basic supplies such as washing liquid, utensils (not even a butter knife), or any way to light the stoves, which barely worked anyway. Thankfully, we had our own gas cooker. There was only one sink available, which caused a line-up even though it wasn’t a busy time of year.

To top it off, the early morning was anything but peaceful, with loud horrific sheep noises right next to our tent. Not quite the outback tranquillity we’d hoped for.

We thought maybe we were just being fussy, but after chatting with other guests, it seemed many shared the same frustrations. Unfortunately, it just didn’t live up to expectations.

We spent 5 nights in a superior 2 bedroom unit.
Pluses:
The view from our cabin esp at sun rise n sunset were spectacular.
Hot showers
Comfortable bed
Great walking trails
Location to attractions of flinders ranges
Woodshed restaurant
Minuses:
Cabins were basic but clean
Kitchen only suited for simple meals and breakfasts with minimal pots and crockery
Cabins were not serviced. Towels changed every 2nd day only
Convenience store was really inadequate. You have to go to Wilpena pound iga. For fresh bread etc.
The customer service is absolutely fantastic. Room very clean and the food and service from the restaurant is out of this world
I stayed in the campground / bush camp area and it was beautiful
Great sites, good ablution blocks, well located

Rawnsley Park  Station.
An extremely  busy park, I was surprised with the number of people, especially  after 2 weeks of off grid camping in Flinders.
Was big enough you were not in each other's pockets.
Showers in old brick block great, showers in the tin showers block not as good.
Laundry was great and good sized washing lines.
The camping kitchen was large space. Not enough tables and chairs on windy wet days.
Petrol pumps a great bonus.
Internal roads to reach off grid camping area towards and past second toilet was pretty rough going, especially  when staying multiple days back an forwards.
Great to see Emus wandering through our site, felt sorry for the sheep they seemed frightened  by the people and vehicles.
Internal walks were great.
Fantastic  views of Rawnsley  Bluff.
Nice restaurant, by warned you need to order take away by 5 30p don't get caught out!

Loved that they allow you to wash your vehicles for a donation to Flying doctors.
The Arkapema Track is well worth the money, take a picnic lunch loads of places to stop and enjoy the environment. We spent 7 hours doing the track, took our time and thoroughly  enjoyed the serenity.
The shop had basic supplies, i bought a few items and the next day visited the IGA at Wilpena Pound and found items much cheaper, so Rawnsley shop is good for convenience.
The road leading into Rawnsley  Park Station is like a race track, the speed people drive, giving there are people walking, mountain  bike riders, vehicles pulling in and out of restaurant area, the emus, was really not necessary. An emu was hit on that Internal road when wecwere there.  Even if Rawnsley on there main riad access road reduced the speed limit from dusk to dawn would be a great start..
Such beautiful surroundings in this idyllic location can quickly spoil by grumpy owners/managers.

Booked the Highlights package to celebrate a special WA. Stayed in an Eco Villa (amazing), did the 8hr 4WD tour, but sadly the 30min flight over the Flinders was cancelled due to a plane issue (full refund provided immediately by reception).
If you want something very special (though not cheap) this is the way to go. However, the park offers every kind of accommodation from free camping sites to cabins, and they are in differest areas so you really do feel the openess of the area.
The whole process from booking, confirmation emails, relevant information on our stay, arrival (reception staff friendly & knowledgeable), to checking out was so professional and smooth.
Our 4WD Tour guide (Rachel) was super friendly and easy going, knowledgeable and you could just tell she loves working there and we were all amazed to see the wildlife and the landscape.
It is close to Wilpena Pound (NP Day Pass $10), Hawker 30mins south, the Gorges (easy 4WD roads), so many lookouts, and Blinman (have to taste the pies at Blinman Hotel) is only an hours drive north, as well as so many walking tracks on the property itself.
So just book a stay, you wont be disappointed.

We camped in the free camping area. So good. Lots of spaces, not on top of each other, and those views. Woow, woow, woow. Yes, the amenities block was busy at times, yes it was dusty, but... just look where you are. The staff in the office (whose names I didn't get) were exceptional, giving us advice on what to see and do, plus helped with a quick trip back to Hawker for a little medical help. Thank you ladies. We will be encouraging everyone we meet to stay at Rswnsley Park.
Nice place to stay. Good hot showers.
A classic flinders ranges camping spot- great location, lots of walking trails (marked/map) good facilities and great bush camping - dog friendly - repeat visit - always great!

Stayed in unpowered camp site, good amenities.
Great helpful staff.
Dog sitting available when you want to go into National Park.
Dog was very well looked after.

First visit to Rawnsley Park station.  Arrived very late for the first night, so did not see much.  The late check in went smoothly and we awoke to a spectacular view from our 2 bedroom superior cabin. Did a 2 day flight with Chinta Air - amazing - and spent another night in a 1 bedroom cabin (3 of us) on our return.  We also had booked dinner at the Woolshed restaurant. The staff during the phone bookings, changes to our booking and adding a second night were extremely helpful and I felt confident they had all things under control.  The setting is superb and I would love to go back and spend additional time there, exploring the area.  We were fortunate to be there after a good rain, so no dust, flies, gorgeous weather and amazing scenery with lush green growth.  The cabins were pretty clean (for being in the bush), beds comfortable, nice bathrooms, clean linen, toiletries provided, coffee, tea etc.  I certainly hope to return and stay for several days, sometime in the future.
Great Outback campsite. We have powered site. Amenities are  new and clean.

Wonderful price through the Flinders Ranges to get here.
Magnificent.
Nice camping great facilities

Camped out.
Great experience.
Very helpful staff. Well setup and maintained. Amazing scenery. Lots of travellers here I was surprised by the sheer number of vans and people

Great place as an entry to the Ranges. Lots of accommodation options. Food is good at the restaurant as well.
The reception staff could be a bit more friendly.
There are lots of activities around and it has some very nice views at sunrise or sunset.

Very quiet place to get away if you’re looking to escape the big cities, good camp kitchen and shop has some goods. Essential you bring firewood from outside the park as you can’t cut it and is quite expensive. The woodshed restaurant amazing food and great service.

A fantastic place to stay to access a wide variety of Flinders Ranges experiences - from walks on your doorstep, to Wilpena Pound in the national park, to scenic drives through the park (both 4 and 2WD accessible). On top of this, there is a shop with some supplies including wine and a very good restaurant (Woolshed). The accommodation we stayed in (studio cabin with en-suite) was perfect for our needs.

Great setting in Ikara! A wonderful natural environment in Australia’s backyard. Stayed in a motorhome in the caravan park for a week relaxing and exploring the area. Plenty of flora and fauna to learn about and enjoy. Staff are friendly and helpful. The Woolshed restaurant is fantastic with a local natural vibe to the food. (Watch out for the access road, she’s a bit rough!)

Rawnsley Park Stations cancellation policy is strict. Absolutely no refunds or credits after booking, even after asking because of a last minute cancellation due to a medical emergency. I think they could be a bit more sympathetic and allow at the very least a credit for future stays. I would advise travel insurance if staying maybe longer than one night. Our other booked accommodations in the area gave us full refunds even though they didn’t have to. Disappointing and will look to stay elsewhere in future. I also work in a tourist business and will be advising prospective Flinders Ranges travellers of this. Even Booking.com plead our situation and circumstances and still no response from RP Station.
Rude !!!

We recently spent 11 nights here, 8 on a powered sites, then 3 on a bush camp.
We have been twice before and absolutely love it here. It is so central to everything and the walking tracks are great. Dog friendly which is a big plus. Just such beautiful surroundings. Staff are helpful and amenities are clean.
Thanks for a great stay.
Best central location. Hiked up the Bluff which gave me amazing views of Wilpena Pound and of Rawnsley park. Woolshed for dinner is the best lamp I've ever had and definitely worth the price

Have only stayed on powered sites plenty of room view's where ever you look will be returning in a couple of months
Great place to base yourself when exploring the Flinders
Great location to explore the Flinders Rangers

There's a bit of a debate
Stay at Wilpena or Rawnsley.
The views of the range are much better at Rawnsley. There's a much bigger camping area the facilities even in the remote camp are great and plenty of special private camps.
Wilpena can get crowded and there are lots of trees. Rawnsley is a bit barren. In Rawnsley the view is superb
Lovely stay at Rawnsley Park Station. Rooms were clean and comfortable and there were lots of wonderful hikes available. Highly recommended.

More like a family caravan park than a bush retreat.

Line up to check in was long, waiting in the car along the road waiting with no information as to what was happening was a bit disconcerting. Would be great to have received communication from the park outlining what to expect upon arrival especially in peak season but communication isn’t a strong suit.

Bathrooms and cooking facilities were there and were as clean as can be with large groups of families constantly moving in and out. I am unaware if they had been cleaned when we stayed.

Would recommend avoiding amenities at busy times as there are big groups that make for long wait times - being mornings and night…

Pool is very small and is very much for children, as is most other public spaces. Unless you have children, expect to spend most of your downtime at the Station at your campsite. A bit of a disappointment as after a day hike a swim in the pool would have been a nice option.

Expect to listen to layers of music and personal TVs throughout the night, with a lot of light pollution from a range of caravans and RVs with fairly lights and assortments of external lights as you sit by your campfire.

This is not for nature lovers; or people that want to immerse themselves in nature. Lights and sound of other campers will, most likely take away from an experience of the dark sky and nature if that’s what you were after.

The few nights we stayed, we had the pleasure of watching a passive aggressive stand off between different campers ‘turning lights off’ and ‘turning lights on’ in the recreation BBQ area, with different campers having different priorities.

People at reception weren’t particularly friendly, as it was our first time in the area and hoping the Flinders Rangers would be a regular holiday spot, we were keen to learn about where to go and what to do, it was unfortunate but volunteers, staff and rangers at the further along Wilpena Pound were fantastic and so helpful in guiding us.

Following the saying ‘if you can’t beat them, join them’ I would highly recommend Rawnsley Park Station for large, loud groups, drinkers, parties, and big families a great place for children and people that want to get away, but bring their TV and mod-cons with them!

Unfortunately, this accommodation wasn’t what we were looking for, and found it hard to find information on what it would be!

The Woodshed is a great place to escape for quiet/adult(ish) time if need be!

We went out the back of the property to free camp area, lovely & quiet, the cost is a bit high when you are not using any facilities. The 4WD self guided tour was great & the Woolshed restaurant was lovely on a cold night. Food & service was great.
We caravaned. Loved the site. Well appointed and breathtaking views. The dawns were beautiful.

Absolutely magical experience! Visiting Wilpena Pound has been on my bucket list for years, and I finally made it there for my birthday — it did not disappoint! We stayed at Rawnsley Park Station in one of their incredible Eco Villas, and all I can say is WOW! Everything was immaculate and beautifully presented — it felt like our own private piece of paradise.

The continental breakfast was a generous and delicious spread with croissants, fresh bread, yoghurt, fruit, and juice — such a perfect way to start the day. We even had kangaroos right outside our villa, including sweet little joeys with their mums — an absolute highlight!

The scenery was nothing short of breathtaking, and I genuinely didn’t want to leave. We’re already planning our next visit — can’t wait to come back! Highly, highly recommend!

Excellent staff and epic off site camping. Took our 4WD around their tracks which were worth doing as well. Lovely newly renovated bathroom facilities.
Love the new manager Kim ❤️ you’re the best !!

We had an incredible stay here with our dogs in one of the dog friendly cabins. The station is in a great location close to the park but also with so many great hikes and amenities of its own. The cabins were small but cosy and made for a great stay, only suggestion would be enclosing the balconies with a gate to allow dogs some off leash time. The hikes were incredible on the property as well as the 4wd track which is a must do and well maintained. Flinders is somewhere you should go at least once and I’d highly recommend staying at Rawnsley!

We stopped for the night on a moto Tour - dinner at the restaurant was fantastic - lamb shank was fantastic.
Staff were superb , friendly , new manager Alisha - was fantastic , she greeted us, and was super helpful , restaurant was busy , the team were very busy keeping up , some but well conducted by the GM - who guided orders in a timely fashion, so the chef could get all meals on tables in timely fashion.

Great experience - friendly team, very pleased with food

Room was facing out to the ranges - great set up -

I stayed twice in this station. First in the caravan park the second time in the bush. Both time the same experience : noise! shouts, loud voices and music! Not an experience of peace, quiet and immersion in nature. Not recommended for nature lovers. Just a vast circus.
Great views and you really felt like your camping in the ranges. Facilities were good

What a fantastic place to stay. Absolutely loved the woolshed restaurant. My lamb shank meal was the biggest shank I have ever had. Menu was great. The restaurant had a great atmosphere and there was happy hour just before our dinner booking. Honestly can’t fault the rooms, restaurant or location

Fabulous location base for the Wilpena hikes. Stayed in eco villas which are fabulous; amenities, breakfast foods excellent, furnishings comfortable... great bath for post hike relax. Near by swimming pool. Dinner dining just to die for.... recommend lamb dishes. Local lamb have eat salt bush YUM, tender. Veg fresh desserts sweet and delish.  All Staff AMAZINGLY EXCELLENT Wonderful stay thank you

We actually camp out in the back paddock. You can drive around and pick out the perfect spot for you. We love the colours on the rock as the sun goes down. Perfect. Peaceful. Our fav place to camp in the Flinders
We did two tours here and ate at the restaurant. Recommend, staff and tour guides are lovely

Great stepping-off point for Flinders Ra adventures. Great, clean, spacy and comfy units. Airtours - helo and fixed-wing - available. GREAT restaurant. 4WD twilight canapé tour was fun. Hard to fault this place.
Fabulous place to stay, great cabins and amazing views. Would recommend to everyone.

We did not stay here but did the 4hr 4WD morning tour with Shane. That guy is an amazing font of useful and interesting information - incredibly educational trip made highly entertaining with his knack for story telling and personality- highly recommend a tour with him, just ask him a lot of questions- you won’t regret it!
Great spot, excellent staff and service with a great restaurant

Located on the south eastern edge of Wilpena Pound, Rawnsley Park Station adjoins the Flinders Ranges National Park, 420 kilometres from Adelaide, a comfortable five hour drive on sealed roads.

Stay on an operating sheep station - experience the natural beauty and wildlife of this ancient rugged landscape with a choice of accommodation styles including secluded bush camping, powered camping/caravan sites, shearers quarters sleeping 20, budget cabins, one, two and three bedroom self-contained holiday units, secluded four and a half star, straw-bale construction Eco-villas and Rawnsley Homestead.

Facilities include a shop, selling food, alcohol and souvenirs, petrol, diesel, outdoor swimming pool and Woolshed Restaurant.

All rooms with kitchen, air conditioning, television, free public wifi. Telstra & Optus mobile signal is available on the property

There are a range of walking and cycle trails, four wheel drive tours with qualified and experienced guides, take a scenic flight or helicopter flight. Speak to their helpful staff about what you can do and see during your stay.

Wonderful place with amazing views
The checkin information could have been explained better. But a top notch place. So many kangaroos , emus , birds ( galahs and ring neck parrots). Highly recommend. The pool is beautiful too with an amazing views of 330 million years old rockfalls. Super unique.

A lovely spot off the beaten track, very nice chalet stocked with a generous welcome pack.
Covered car port (the Kangaroos like to use).
Good bed and shower, decking out front with stunning views.
Room sevice and restocked on second day.
Swimming pool on site, and a restaurant.
Only down side is some staff don't observe the speed limit on site, otherwise top notch stay.

We booked a bush camping spot for 3 nights. Were able to mover every night around the 5 acres and were nowhere near anyone. But still near a clean flushing toilet. If I could rate more than 5 stars I would. (Side note, to the person who drank the bore water here and got sick despite all the signs saying ‘don’t drink the water’, you are the reason there is warning labels on bleach).

Come to Rawnsley Park Station for the scenery and station amenities, they’re exceptional. We had emus walk right near the outdoor pool, which was very neat. Our 3 bedroom cabin was spacious for group of 7 (we had an accessible unit) but quite outdated and it had quite a bit of ants traveling through it. The cabin could definitely use some TLC and updating as I believe it was built in the early 1990s and looks the part.

The pool was right in front of our cabin which was convenient and had a beautiful view of Rawnsley Bluff. The Woolshed Restaurant offers both dine in and takeaway and I had the latter - chicken parm, which was delicious.
Nice spot in the Flinders.. has everything you need. Had dinner at woodshed a bit $$ but we'll worth it for the lamb share  plate

We stayed in a cabin on the campsite and it was great. The cabins are well equipped and the campsite shop is well stocked.
We also went on a 4x4 tour and our guide Rachel was amazing, it was a lot of fun and made our stay in Flinders even better!

4 nights in a camper van. Remarkably clean amenities, nice view on the mountains, plenty of nice walking trails around, beautiful night sky, small shop on site (with a special highlight - tasty local lamb meat for barbecue), and overall pleasant vibe. Higly recommended!
Exceptional 5-night getaway. Guided 4WD as well sunset tours both highly recommended.
Fantastic place to spend a few days while exploring the Flinders. Woolshed Restaurant was also great for dinner!

Had a good time. They need to think about where they place people in the camp area. I'm older and I was at top of hill. Had to walk up and down every time I needed to go to the loo. Also lucky we packed 2 electric cords as we wouldn't have reached to plug in. We didn't get water to our van as the tap was so far from our van. Think it's over priced for what you get. I won't be staying there next time. Will be looking for better stays.

Our small group loved our 2 night bush camp at Rawnsley Park Bush campsite. There were atleast 500 campers during this labour- day long weekend, but the common facilities were quite adequate, spread out and in excellent condition at all times. Hot showers, flushable toilets ,sinks, laundry facilities, fire-pits,outdoor taps and efficient garbage disposal were accessible to all powered and un-powered site. All campers were respectful of each other and the environment. Booking was super easy and the host Tony was always very prompt and helpful with providing accurate information to guests. The site was so spacious and had many beautiful location options for everyone. There's a small lake overlooking the magnificent mountain ranges where huge herds of sheep come to water every dawn and dusk . Many of the un-powered sites have the benefit of watching the mesmerising sunsets on the ranges, as these sites are further into the bush from the entrance to the campsite and also closer to the lake. The best times to visit during the year would be probably during Autumn and Spring months, and definitely avoid the fire-ban season because camping is not 'camping' without a campfire! We will recommend this campsite to anyone and will go again ourselves. Thank you to the Rawnsley Park station team!
The best spot, clean toilets, kitchen! Highly recommended

Just spent 5 days, thoroughly loved all of it.
Magnificent views, great location for day trips to Blinman, Parachilna etc.
I was surprised at what facilities were here imagined it to be more basic. Camped in the bush camp area, plenty of room, loved all the sheep just strolling through camp. Staff are lovely. Great hike up to Rawnsley Bluff. Did the helicopter over Wilpena Pound. Lamb platter for 2 at the Wool Shed was sensational. Big thumbs up from me😄

Not enough stars to give. The scenery is spectacular, the facilities were clean with plenty of showers and toilets. Washing machines available. Nice big camp kitchen. Numerous walking tracks of varying  difficulties. Fire pits are at the sites, and wood is available from the store. The store has basic provisions and souvenirs and ice creams. Pool on site. Just a brilliant site and the drives around Rawnsely are easy and picturesque. Heaps of wildlife abound the area. One of our top places we have visited so far. Aeroplane ride over the pound was well worth the money. The young lass in the shop was a great help. Fuel at a reasonable price, and dump points on site.
8 stars.
Prachtige locatie, we werden verwend in de eco villa Acacia.

Just spent 3 nights in free camping . AWESOME park . Great showers . Flushing toilets throughout park. Downside . We need a generator at times to give us power .. generators can only operate between 8 am & 4 pm . Most evenings we did not get back to our campsite until 6 pm .. I believe Rawnsley should increase their generator operating hrs like other parks till 8 pm

My wife and I were travelling with another couple and stayed in one of the 2 bedroom eco villas.   Each bedroom has its own ensuite.   The villa is modern and well equipped for the independent traveller.   It is spacious with large dining, living and kitchen areas.   There is a large verandah with BBQ overlooking the southern ridge of Wilpena Pound.    The king size bed was comfortable, showers were good, wifi is OK.   The pool is located adjacent to the villas - the water was cold in October but very refreshing after a day of activity in the Flinders Ranges.   It’s luxury living for a remote location and not cheap.

Just giving people a heads up. We booked 3 nights here for a powered site and when we got there the fella  said he didn't have us on the books. He was quite certain I was the one in the wrong as he said if we'd booked a powered site we always take a deposit. I had written everything down for our trip with accommodation and remember the phone call with the lady. She had told me the price and said just pay when you get here. The fella could've said Hey I'm really sorry this has happened and tried to work out something but instead was quick to put the blame on me and said this wouldn't of happened. We looked at a cabin which was $150 for the night and only after asking him he said it doesn't have an ensuite so we have ended up in Hawker in a lovely cabin with and ensuite for the same price. I don't mind if mistakes happen it's the way they are handled. Also talking to people in Hawker it seems to happen to a few people.

Great place to stay. We set up out in the unpowered bush camping section right on the side of the dry creek bed. It was only a short walk to the toilets from our spot. Don’t forget the Woolshed restaurant, the food is top notch.

What a spot to spend a few nights! Campsites and facilities are fantastic, and the lamb sausages 🤤

Enjoyed dinner at the Woolshed one night. Fantastic staff and very attentive and service was fantastic.

4wd loop track was enjoyable too. Lots to see and do.

Thank you team. Look forward to visiting again soon!

Rawnsley Park Station is a great spot to base yourself and explore part of the Flinders Rangers.  The helicopter flight is a must. You get to appreciate how big Wilpena Pound actually is. The Razorback drive has amazing scenery & well worth the drive.

My wife and I spent 3 nights here and had the best time. The friendly and lovely KIm greeted us and was very helpful. Thank you. We had dinner at the restaurant and it was delicious! The most tender lamb. Room 20 was clean and comfortable. It had everything we needed and the breakfast was great. Highly recommend

So close to everything.
Great walks. Some nice and easy.Some very challenging.
Unbelievable beauty if you notice.

Fantastic authentic Australian experience, Rawnsley Park Station has something for everyone, bush camping, powered camp sites, caravan and camper sites, cabins, bunkhouse, luxury cabins and luxury eco villas. Bush walks on the station are spectacular, helicopter and Cessna flights are available to see the unique Flinders Ranges from above. The Woolshed restaurant is an award wining dining option on the site if the old Wool-shed. RPS is a working sheep station so you have to close gates and be aware of signage across the huge site. It’s an amazing place to stay and be, no matter what your accommodation preference is.
Amazing experience in an amazing location
Lovely caravan park. Nice staff, kitchen is well equipped and warm, bathrooms are clean. Highly recommend :)

We were looking forward to our stay at Rawnsley Park but we’re really quite disappointed. We booked for 4 nights but left after 3. Amenities were cleaned once while we were there, but generally are in a terrible state - the bottom of hand drier is like a Petri dish that must never have been wiped clean. Felt like we were imposing when we checked in. Also had a random dog wandering the park for 2 of the days we were there. Generally disappointing, pretty poor value for money and just feels like there’s a lack of care.
Great place to stay and explore the surrounding area. Good clean facilities.

Thank you Rawnsley Park Station for opening up your property. We had 3 nights in the bush camping area and could have easily stayed more! As a solo hiker, I really appreciated the well signed hikes on the property. Flushing toilets were a bonus in the bush camping area!!
There is something special about this place. We planned to stay for 2 nights but ended up staying 4 in the bush camping area as our kids just couldn't get enough.

Great Caravan site and lovely setting
There are some very good walks as well
The meat that can be bought at their store is A1 quality and very very tastie
Lovely place to camp for a day or two
Lots of sites. Dirt road in. Drive slowly. Access to plane and helicopter trips was easy.
Stayed 4 nights bush Camping, best spot in the flinders in my opinion. Quiet relaxing and well spaced out. Super clean amenities and great staff

Check in was smooth and quick. The room we booked was clean and comfortable. I loved the kitchen with full stove so we could eat in with groceries we brought. They even leave fresh ground coffee for you to make in the mornings with a French press! The bed was the most comfortable with best quality sheets I’ve slept on this whole trip. If you are trying to decide if it’s worth the price to stay here, it is. You are so close to the national park and lots of hikes and important ancient sites. FYI if you want to eat at a restaurant for dinner you need to book the Woolshed restaurant very far in advance!!
COMPLETELY UNSEALED AND SERIOUS CORRUGATIONS IN DRIVEWAY.

We stayed in a more budget cabin but for the low price it was just awesome. The views are incredible and the staff were very nice. Definitely recommend, we also did a couple of the walks and they were incredible

amazing views of wilpena pound especially in the bush camp section which was very spacious. It was a fantastic base for day tripping. dog friendly with a bonus of doggy day care so we could visit the national park.

We stayed in an Eco Villa and it was stunning. Beautifully clean, modern and well maintained. The setting and views have been captured perfectly. Everything you need is supplied-full kitchen, BBQ, just bring your food supplies. The wrap around verandah with amazing views was so special. There are nice drives and walks nearby. So relaxed after our stay.

Great food too bad if your a vegetarian
I AM NOT.
Did free camping, such a stunning place, great amenities,

I stayed in a cabin that was extremely comfortable.  It was very spacious and had a car port.  The cabin contained everything for my needs.  This is a very central place to stay if exploring the area and it is not necessary to stay at Wilpena Pound. There was a very useful map given to guests that provided bite size chunks of information about local flora and fauna that I found very informative.  The attached photo is the view from my cabin.
Lovely view and lovely cabins

Spacious cabins throughout with amenities and awesome views in the morning. A little hard to see at night with limited lighting around the resort when heading back after dark back to your room so drive/walk slow.
Lovely spot friendly staff.  Dirt road in but if you drive slower it’s fine.  Lovely walks and sign posted
Fabulous station stay in the beautiful Flinders Rangers, we stayed 3 nights wish we'd stayed longer 💚🤎

We stayed on a powered caravan site and found Rawnsley Park Station very good. The only thing I did notice was some sites could be challenging for caravans to reverse into but overall the sites were of a good size and the facilities were also very good and supported by friendly staff. It is a great place to stay when exploring the Flinders Ranges.

Spectacular destination in every way possible,.
Fully operational sheep station dedicated to providing the ultimate destination for giving us the opportunity to base ourselves for exploring all the Flinders Ranges region has to offer.
The location, staff, amenities, facilities, camp sites for powered and unpowered or off grid are exceptional, with camp kitchens, laundry facilities, walking trails and scenic flights that can also be booked through the station caravan park, a truly complete experience.

We booked a sunrise flight over Wilpena Pound that was absolutely stunning with our Pilot Corey, providing an exceptional knowledge based commentary throughout the entire flight.
In short we must highly recommend this part of the Flinders Ranges experience if you have the time.
I have not added pictures of the flight on purpose, as you just have to experience this for yourself.

Unfortunately these guys put us through a lot to get a refund on our booking when we had to cancel threw a few unfortunate events.
We had to cancel 10 days out and was told numerous times was not refundable, it was only that I persisted and persisted that we were eventually not charged.
I would never question it if there was no availability or things had been done to accommodate us or if there was a administration fee that would have been fine!
But as this was not peak season and there were plenty of cabins available to book, so we were not taking from bookings that would have been, I saw this as a total money grab!
We have got everything in order now and going past on our previously plan trip though we will not be staying.
The other bookings we had for the month trip did everything in their power to help, these guys are the only ones that did not!
Excellent the food left for breakfast would have feed 4!! And was lovely. good bed and shower beautiful location good restaurant

We just did the Arkapeena 4WD track - just go to reception and get the keys. One for the first part which is good 3-4 hours of maybe medium 4WD track. Did it while it was spitting rain and it was fine. Didn’t end up doing to “red” low range track but that will be for another day. Great service and highly recommended.

Highly recommend the superior units, our 2 bed-room unit was clean, modern and had great views of the ranges! The pool was so refreshing after a day’s hike and also great views. We say kangarooos and emus wondering around from the pool and just from our balcony/patio. Lovely sitting outside just for the views.
Location was central for all the attractions. walking  trails and also a short walk to the woolshed restaurant.
We booked via luxury escapes and included continental breakfast provisions which was plenty for our stay!

We loved our stay here at Rawnsley Park, the room was super clean, spacious and great kitchen. The location was amazing with Lots of great hikes nearby.

Waking up for sunrise was perfect! The view was amazing. It was so nice and quiet during the nights.

Service was great as well, very friendly staff. We would definitely come back here!

Highly recommend getting up early for sunrise hikes and seeing the sunset, the colours were spectacular!

Our recent stay at Rawnsley Park Station was fantastic! The welcoming staff, well-maintained grounds, and spacious caravan site made for a delightful experience. The clean facilities, beautiful natural surroundings, and thoughtful amenities added to the overall charm. We highly recommend them for a rejuvenating getaway and look forward to returning in the future!
Gut gelegener CP für Besuch der Flinders Ranges. Sehr nettes Personal, grosse Campkitchen (nicht offen), WC und Duschen sauber

Absolutely Amazing Place ,In the middle of the Ranges .Highly Facilited Amenities .Swimming pools ,friendly staffs and The Tent Camping spot was beautifully maintained .Flushable Toilets .Shower. laundry stands .kitchen everything was well maintained .You have Access of everything you want over there . Few Birds and Red ants might come around but its nature cant complain Thanks Team Rawnsleyfor everything  ❤️❤️🙏🙏
Such an incredible stay. We had our caravan but the bathrooms were amazing and clean, pool sublime and staff super friendly. It was our best and most peaceful stop

Unpowered caravan site was excellent. Nice and level and great views. Private but close enough to the great shower and toilet block. Plenty of hot water and very clean. Would definitely recommend and stay again.

There are good facilities for caravan/campers and great hikes within the park. They will mind your dog for the day for a fee if you wish to visit national parks so we have really enjoyed ourselves here and hope you do too.
Great spot  very friendly and accommodating staff, will come back.

I stayed at Rawnsley Park a couple of months back on a camping trip with friends that was an experiment post a brain injury I suffered some years back.  It went wrong for me due to my disability and I had to make an emergency call to Rawnsley Park management to try and change my accommodation.  They had no spare bookings available for alternative accommodation but were very creative in coming up with an alternative and helping me solve the issue I was having. My alternative was getting to Adelaide and travelling back to Melbourne via plane/train which I was seriously considering. Good old-fashioned service, and a little thinking outside of the box turned a disaster into a great experience and I cannot thank the staff enough.
Friendly group of locals visited us and chatted and chirped the time away.

Fabulous location within the Flinders Ranges. Has well marked walking trails making the tracks easily distinguishable. Accommodation is nice and comfortable. Has good swimming pools with an amazing backdrop and a restaurant on site which serves up some excellent food. Had everything I was looking for and thoroughly enjoyed. The setting is stunning such that sunrise and sunset are events in themselves. Plenty of wildlife on the walks.

Wunderschöne Häuschen „Eco-Villas“ mit Blick in den Sternenhimmel und auf die naheliegende Bergkette. Essen kann man im zur Anlage gehörenden Wool Shed Restaurant (Wichtig: Möglichst frühzeitig im Voraus reservieren)

Great place to stay when visiting the Flinders Ranges. Friendly management. Unpowered camp sites with the best view of Wilpena Pound. Pick your own spot. There are lots of them!! Powered sites are good as well. Good amenities block. Dog friendly. We paid $27 per night with Gday park discount for unpowered. Felt it was good value. There are lookouts and walks to do without leaving the park. Handy for dog owners as you don't have to worry about being in a national park. They offer dog minding as well. Enjoyed our 5 day stay here.
Great spot and staff were excellent and helpful..👍

What a truly amazing place to be so fortunate to visit.  I wish I had been able to stay longer.
