The accommodation is perfect, 2 bedroom cabins with full self catering facilities but if you are like me and prefer to let someone else do all the work for you, dinner at the Woolshed is the best.  I had the kangaroo and would which was cooked superbly.  So tender.

Be sure to check out the Flinders Gin as well.  A great starter followed by some local red wine.

Topped of my visit with a scenic flight over the pound with great commentary.  1 hour cruising at 2km up gave me the best view and was well worth the investment.

Now I just need to work out when I can get back for another visit.

A great spot about 30kms from Wilpena Pound. Fabulous views of Rocks & ranges.
Clean well presented accommodation. Polite helpful staff. Local walks.
Campground caravan Park conveniently located for the southern Flinders Ranges SA
Excellent set up here. Looking forward to exploring this Flinders Ranges area over next few days.
Definitely the best place to stay in Flinders Ranger.
Simply stunning

Amazing that Rawnsley Park saw the opportunity to share their part of the Flinders with paying guests.  Great park, lots of walking trails and a base to explore the Flinders from.  Pool was refreshing with a great back drop!

Have given a five but only just, so here are the two butts…..!
No doubt the park has incrementally expanded over the years.  Wherever we walked in and around the cabins, there was the oppressive smell of under performing septic systems.  Lastly the pillows in the cabin were over due to be tossed out as they smelled badly of body hair oils.  The linen was fresh and couldn’t fault anything else.  Breakfast provisions were generous.  Would definitely stay again but would bring my own pillow.
Good camping for exploring the Flinders ranges, unfortunately was let down by dirty amenities, we stayed 3 days no body cleaned them in this time,yuk

Very popular camping ground situated on a working station.  Everything was booked out when we arrived. So we ended up in a non powered site too far from wifi.  Campers were very friendly and well behaved. Lots of kids running around. The restaurant was booked out so I cannot comment.  All amenities were clean and in good order.
One night unpowered 3 nights powered changed to 4 nights unpowered as lovely quiet spot with magic views

We spent 6 nights here in the bush camping area. Everything about Rawnsley Park exceeded our expectations and our stay was the highlight of our 7 week road trip from Tasmania to south west WA and back. Sitting on the deck at The Woolshed with a cold beer looking up at Rawnsley Bluff at sunset is an especially great experience. We’re already planning our return next year! And a big thank you to the hard-working staff who do a fantastic job.
Our second stay, first was in a cabin, this time camping on a powered site. Great staff, good pricing all round. Lovely staff and food at the woodshed. Thank you!

The cabins were spacious with most comfortable beds and pillows, squeaky clean, came with luxurious towels and linen.
Juxtaposed in the valley are the cluster of cabins with a swimming pool, the Woolshed Restaurant and further inwards are beautiful eco villas sandwiched by the caravan park that stretches up the hillside.
The Wool Shed restaurant had stunning meals, beautifully and perfectly cooked roo, goat stew amongst other dishes.
Desserts were not shout out loud good like the meals, cooked breakfasts were a disaster with cold dark toast that taste like sawdust and over cooked scrambled eggs. However,  muesli bowls of fruit compote and yougurt were acceptable.
Great accessibility to hikes nearby and the restaurant balcony is the place to be at sunset.
A short hike up Saddleback towards sunset is also recommended for stunning views.
Stayed here for 4 nights in a unpowered site up on a hill, we had a magnificent outlook to the Ranges, sun rises to remember

We stayed on a powered caravan site overlooking the flowing creek.  As usual the caravan park was heavily booked during our stay which is a reflection of the great service and high standards of the park.  The amenities were clean and serviced daily.  Looking forward to returning next year but in July

Nestled in the Flinders Rangers, Rawnsley Park Station is one of my favourite spots to visit. It had little to no service which meant time spent talking and laughing with family around the fire. The bathrooms were clean and we were able to get firewood there aswell. The views from any spot camping, were spectacular. The animals around the camp were plenty and I definitely can’t fault it, worth the time and effort of going there!
I stayed four nights and loved the park.

Our first visit to Flinders Ranges was enhanced by our stay at the Rawnsley Park Station Caravan Park. Just loved that we could bush camp but still have access to amenities.
Found the Reception staff and information (brochures, maps etc) very helpful.
It was an ideal location for us and would happily book there again.
Great place to stay. Good sites and amenities- picturesque,Friendly staff - looking forward to another visit
Absolutely sensational.
Good Caravan Park, great walks and beautiful scenery.
Caravan park accommodation for us. Great camp and location. Fuel and shop on site. Easy travel into Flinders Ranges and many other interesting drives.

We had a great stay for 7 nights on a powered site. The amenities were clean and the staff were friendly and helpful. We also did 2 guided tours, which took us to very special places, including seeing lots of rock wallabies. Our guide, Beth, was very knowledgeable and made the tours memorable. Thank you

We booked a powered camp site.
The site was rather slim, and we had very friendly neighbours who wanted to constantly chat.
Would have been nicer to have more space, or be further away from other campers.
Overall we had a great experience here and would definitely come back, we'll just request a site further away if possible.

Stayed at the caravan park.
Amazing staff.
Super clean.
......and they looked after the dog

Powered site, great scenery, walking trails.
Fantastic, helpfull staff, great trip. Thankyou
Great bush camping with all the services you need.
One night stay and an amazing experience
Incedible views!
Great place to stay when visiting the Southern Flinders

We camped in the non powered sites in the bush camp. You could park anywhere and lots of room so not next to anyone. Showers were great. Laundry was handy but only one dryer as others out of order. Would stay again

I have been going to Rawnsley Park for many years now, and this was the first time I've been disappointed with my visit. We booked a cabin, and on first arrival, i noticed that the cabins had been relocated. There was a huge lineup of people trying to book in. Turns out the park was overbooked, and there were people absolutely everywhere. The cabins that once looked out at the Wilpena Pound had been moved to another area where the benefits were area was more secluded but no view.

When dealing with staff and getting key to the cabin. They were fantastic. i can not speak more highly of the two working under pressure and still managed to be so accomdating and friendly.

The new cabin location some bright spark decided to put the firepit about 3 metres from the cabin door entrance so have a guess which direction the smoke decided to travel in...yup even with minimal opening and closing of the cabin door everything smelt soo badly of smoke.

The biggest disappointment is the fact that the Woolshed Restaurant located on the property no longer serves their famous lamb pizza. Given that it's a sheep station, it was a part of the experience eating meat from the property and let me tell you something it was no word of a lie the greatest pizza i have ever eaten and would easily drive from Adelaide yearly just for the pizza. My partner was disappointed as it was all i kept going on about. Spoke to numerous people around the park who agreed, saying the pizza needs to be put back on the menu regardless of who the current chef is. What made things worse was that the pizza was smaller and was not cooked. The base wasn't cooked at all yet edges burnt. We dont take things back, so 2 minute noodles it was for dinner.

We ended up having campers right behind us because they had overbooked and had random people walking along our front steps of our cabin because it was in the was of a walking trail.

The cabin we stayed in leaked on the kitchen bench due to rain or plumbing came through wall and it was where my phone and keys were sitting so they sat in a heap of water all night i only realised when i woke the next morning.

This is a lengthy review because Rawnsley Park station was my favourite place in the world, and i wanted my partner to experience that same feeling i get going to this station. It didn't happen!
Absolutely amazing place, stunning views, great facilities.
Really nice place to spend a few days, the shop is reasonably priced and has a lot of items. The only thing that could be upgraded is the amount of showers / toilets...
Absolutely a great holiday to be had

We had a good time but sadly the only thing to spoil our time was the near enough CONSTANT yelping/barking of the puppies they were trying to sell, it spoilt our and many other campers evening sitting around the fires just hearing that constantly and waking up to it too early in the morning. If we come back I'll be checking they haven't got puppies. I can only imagine if that noise was coming from one of the camp sites
Great place to stay on your adventure to the Flinders Ranges. We camped as a group and enjoyed the experience.
